failure_logs: The first run rejected legitimate one-decimal mile-marker display text before the runtime formatter was aligned with the map pipeline.
The first clean double export included a pending-review glTF fixture whose imported PackedScene node identifiers varied between fresh Godot import caches.

acceptance_comparisons.criterion: exact Blender, glTF, and Godot import toolchain is pinned and headless
acceptance_comparisons.observed: Blender 5.1.2 build ec6e62d40fa9 and its official Linux archive checksum are pinned; glTF and Godot 4.7.1 profiles are hash-locked; no interactive editor action is required

acceptance_comparisons.criterion: recursive machine-readable authorship, rights, transformation, and redistribution provenance
acceptance_comparisons.observed: schema-1 manifest verifies creation script, source, transformation scripts, profiles, derived assets, wrapper, import settings, contact sheet, and inventories by SHA-256; redistribution remains explicitly pending human review

acceptance_comparisons.criterion: source lint, export, scale, axes, transforms, LOD, collision, materials, textures, and budgets are enforced
acceptance_comparisons.observed: two builds matched exactly; the gate rejected unapplied scale, a missing semantic node, and a linked absolute texture path; the 7.2 by 12 by 0.24 meter fixture stayed within every declared budget

acceptance_comparisons.criterion: official Godot import resolves a project-owned wrapper without build-time release dependencies
acceptance_comparisons.observed: official Godot 4.7.1 resolved five required semantic nodes and the stable automation ID in a validation-only PCK; distributable presets exclude pending-review pipeline fixtures as well as Blender source, data manifests, asset tooling, and test automation

acceptance_comparisons.criterion: deterministic renderer-backed review artifact and structured inventories exist
acceptance_comparisons.observed: three fixed camera views produced a 1152 by 288 contact sheet with exact within-platform repeatability; Blender, Godot, manifest, budget, and hash inventories are durable

acceptance_comparisons.criterion: graybox fallback supports normal source builds without Blender
acceptance_comparisons.observed: the checked-in GLB and wrapper satisfy the same semantic contract; the full repository gate and official Godot smoke passed without invoking Blender

output_artifacts.retention: CI artifact; durable source inventories are stored under data/assets/pipeline-fixtures

failure_logs.failure: The first fixture generator used a retired Eevee enum and assumed a default World in Blender 5.1.
failure_logs.recovery: The exact Blender 5.1 API names and an explicit preview World are now authored by the generator.

failure_logs.failure: Keeping the .blend beside runtime assets made headless Godot attempt to import the build-time source and dirtied the worktree with editor sidecars.
failure_logs.recovery: Build inputs moved under a .gdignore data directory and all Godot import and release-pack work now runs in an isolated project copy.

failure_logs.failure: The first external-texture mutation was not retained as a linked file and therefore did not exercise the portability rejection.
failure_logs.recovery: The mutation now links an actual absolute-path image into the pavement material, and the linter rejects it with a targeted error.

failure_logs.failure: The first Linux contact-sheet run aborted because Blender's headless renderer could not load libEGL.so.1.
failure_logs.recovery: The asset workflow installs the minimal libegl1 runtime and retains renderer-backed evidence as a mandatory gate.

failure_logs.failure: Shared CI runners intermittently exceeded a hard 50 ms first-frame chunk threshold and aborted otherwise valid normal startup.
failure_logs.recovery: Normal runtime records timing without treating scheduler or JIT variance as content corruption; explicit performance profiles retain the declared budget assertions.

failure_logs.failure: The variable-lane fixture emitted realistic one-decimal mile-marker text while the runtime required three-decimal anchor text.
failure_logs.recovery: The runtime now shares the map pipeline's one-decimal sign formatting contract, focused tests cover rounding, and the 45-edge topology profile passes with zero chunk failures.

failure_logs.failure: A clean double export was not byte-identical because fresh Godot glTF imports generated varying PackedScene node identifiers for a pending-review fixture.
failure_logs.recovery: A dedicated validation preset retains semantic import tests, shipping presets exclude every pipeline fixture, the shipping PCK inspector rejects fixture leakage, and two clean Linux release exports now match exactly.

recovery_result: Local deterministic asset, full repository, PlayGodot boundary, variable-lane topology, and clean double-release export gates pass; remote pull-request validation is pending.
